title: Terms of use
metaDescription: REPUTEXA terms of use. SaaS service, Vision Pulse Zenith plans, Stripe billing, termination, force majeure, mediation, French law.
intro: Last updated: April 2026. Version: 1.3. These Terms of Use (“ToU”) form the framework agreement between REPUTEXA and the user of the service. They cover use of the e-reputation platform, review collection (including Zenith), conversational AI tools and omnichannel loyalty features. They apply to any subscription taken out on reputexa.fr, without prejudice to consumers’ mandatory rights. The privacy policy (Loyalty & Reviews version) and legal notice are integral to the contractual documentation. REPUTEXA uses artificial intelligence models: their outputs may be inaccurate or unsuitable; any suggestion produced by the service must be reviewed and validated by the User before sending or publication (see Article 5).

objet_content: These ToU define the terms of access to and use of the REPUTEXA service, an e-reputation SaaS platform. By creating an account or subscribing, you accept these conditions without reservation.
plans_content: REPUTEXA offers several subscription plans (Vision, Pulse, Zenith and possible extensions). Features (review aggregation, AI replies, alerts, reporting, loyalty programme, connected till or terminal, customer file import) vary by plan and activated modules. Detailed description is available on the Pricing page.
langue_title: 3. Differentiation clause — Language of AI replies
langue_content: By contract, the language of AI-generated replies is differentiated by plan: Vision plan: review replies are generated exclusively in the establishment’s language (user profile language). The service does not offer replies in other languages. Pulse and Zenith plans: the service is multilingual; replies are generated natively in the language of the customer’s review (automatic detection of review language and reply in the same language). This clause is integral to the service conditions and may only change if the plan changes.
facturation_content: Prices shown are exclusive of tax (ex. VAT). VAT is calculated and applied automatically by Stripe according to the buyer’s country and applicable rules (intra-EU VAT, local rates, etc.). Payment is collected by Stripe (cards, SEPA, local methods). The subscription is recurring (monthly or annual). Any month started is due; on termination, no pro rata refund for remaining days unless mandatory law or commercial goodwill stated on the site. Invoices are available in the customer area and via the Stripe portal.
responsabilite_content:
REPUTEXA provides software and acts as technical provider and processor within the meaning of the GDPR for processing entrusted by the merchant. The User (Merchant) is solely responsible for the lawfulness of its own processing (legal bases, information and consents, in-store display, loyalty rules) and for content it publishes, validates or transmits. REPUTEXA shall not be liable for the User’s failure to comply with GDPR, ePrivacy or competition law. The service is provided “as is”; REPUTEXA uses reasonable efforts for availability without guarantee of result or absence of error. Except fraud or wilful misconduct, REPUTEXA’s liability is limited to amounts actually paid by the User in the twelve (12) months preceding the event, subject to mandatory law.

Artificial intelligence — nature of the service and limits: REPUTEXA uses third-party AI models to generate automated proposals (including review replies, message drafts). These systems may produce errors, inaccuracies, unsuitable wording or content that does not match the real context or Merchant intent. The User expressly acknowledges this.

AI non-liability: AI-generated content is provided as suggestions only and must be reviewed, adapted and validated by the User before any sending or publication. The Merchant remains solely responsible for texts it distributes and for their effects under consumer, competition and reputation law. Except wilful misconduct or fraud, REPUTEXA shall not be liable for direct or indirect damage resulting from use or distribution of AI suggestions (including factual errors, clumsiness, misunderstandings or potentially inappropriate statements), nor for commercial commitments that may result; the limitations and liability cap in this article apply in full, subject to mandatory law.

Points value: loyalty points have no monetary or cash value. They cannot be converted into cash or refunded. The Merchant may change reward scales at any time, subject to informing customers as required by law. REPUTEXA does not convert points into cash.

Import of third-party data: when using customer file import, the Merchant warrants that data were collected lawfully and that it has the right to transfer them to REPUTEXA. REPUTEXA disclaims liability for import of contact lists without prior consent or another appropriate legal basis.

For end-customer data (review solicitation, messaging, loyalty), the User is controller and REPUTEXA acts on its documented instructions. The User undertakes fair use of channels: respect for the purpose announced to the customer (reviews, loyalty, rewards) and applicable anti-spam rules.

When the User enables team motivation or internal ranking features, it warrants the accuracy of information entered (names or pseudonyms) and informs data subjects beforehand in accordance with employment law and company practice (including representative bodies where applicable). These features are designed as recognition tools (collective goals, rewards), not as a punitive surveillance device; any diverted use for disciplinary purposes is solely the User’s responsibility. Team share links must be limited to authorised persons; they are provided as secure, non-search-indexed links.
droit_content: These ToU are governed by French law. For professionals, in the absence of an amicable settlement, jurisdiction is assigned to the competent courts of Paris, subject to rules on subject-matter jurisdiction. A consumer customer residing in the European Union may bring proceedings in the courts of their place of residence or those of Paris, in accordance with Brussels I bis. For small-claims disputes, local courts may have jurisdiction under French law.
zenith_obligations_title: 7. Zenith plan — Merchant obligations and limitation of liability
zenith_obligations_content:
Activating the Zenith plan and using automated review collection via WhatsApp (“Capture AI”), possibly combined with loyalty messages, requires clear prior information from the Merchant. In accordance with Articles 13 and 14 GDPR and Article L.34-5 of the French Post and Electronic Communications Code, the Merchant must inform its customers (point of sale, online checkout, GTC): (1) collection of the number; (2) purposes (loyalty, rewards, review solicitation via REPUTEXA as applicable); (3) right to object (STOP and possible consequences for the loyalty account). For e-commerce, a separate consent tick may be required before any send. This obligation rests with the Merchant as controller. REPUTEXA, as technical processor, cannot be liable for the Merchant’s failures.

The system includes frequency safeguards and an objection list (STOP). Messages must remain proportionate to stated purposes. AI-drafted suggestions must be validated by the Merchant before sending.
annexe_fidelite_title: Annex — Model customer notice (loyalty, till, WhatsApp)
annexe_fidelite_content:
The text below is a transparency template to insert in your loyalty rules, signup form or till display. Replace [Shop name] with your brand and provide a link to your single privacy policy (ideally the same URL as the REPUTEXA “Loyalty & Reviews” notice). Reminder: have it reviewed by your counsel.

By joining the loyalty programme and providing your data to [Shop name], you are informed that your number and purchase history may be used to manage your points, rewards (tiers, birthday benefits), contact you about your account and, where applicable, invite you to a satisfaction survey, according to our privacy policy.

If you are part of a network of shops operated by the same controller, your benefits may be pooled across those stores unless otherwise stated at the counter.

Loyalty points have no monetary value: they are not convertible into cash or refundable. The shop may adjust the reward scale while informing you as required by law.

When we use WhatsApp (review collection or programme-related messages), the REPUTEXA technical tool may be used for sending. Replying STOP stops messages; full withdrawal may result in closing the loyalty account and loss of unused points, as stated at the counter.

Under the GDPR you have rights of access, rectification, erasure, objection, restriction and portability; contact the details in our privacy policy.
modifications_title: 8. Changes to the ToU and the service
modifications_content: REPUTEXA may amend these ToU, prices or service features. Users will be informed by appropriate means (email, on-site notification, banner). Continued use after notice constitutes acceptance of the new conditions, unless the right to terminate applies under law. In the event of a materially adverse change, the consumer may terminate without charge under legal conditions.
mediation_content: The consumer may refer free of charge to a consumer mediator in accordance with the French Consumer Code, after attempting direct amicable resolution with REPUTEXA. The mediator’s identity will be stated on the site once the scheme is formalised. EU ODR platform: https://ec.europa.eu/consumers/odr/
suspension_title: 10. Suspension, termination and end of subscription
suspension_content: The subscription may be terminated under the terms provided by Stripe and the offers shown at subscription. In case of non-payment, manifestly abusive use of the service (attempting to circumvent limits, compromising security, use contrary to the ToU) or lasting technical impossibility attributable to a third party, REPUTEXA may suspend or terminate access after formal notice where feasible. At the end of the contract, access ceases; merchant account data are processed in accordance with the privacy policy (export, deletion, legal retention). End-customer data processed on behalf of the merchant (Zenith) follow the controller’s instructions within technical feasibility.
force_majeure_content: REPUTEXA shall not be liable for non-performance or improper performance when due to force majeure or an event beyond its reasonable control (widespread Internet infrastructure failure, disasters, conflicts, decisions by authorities or essential providers, large-scale cyber-attacks despite appropriate security). The service may be temporarily interrupted for planned or emergency maintenance; reasonable notice will be given where circumstances allow.
contact_content: For any question about these ToU: contact@reputexa.fr.
